attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Clean. Here's what was actually broken and what I fixed:

**The bugs that prevented paying users from accessing Video Marketing:**

| # | File | Bug | Fix |
|---|------|-----|-----|

| 3 | `TierGate.tsx` props | Prop was named `hasVideoMarketingAddon`, passed the wrong field | Renamed to `hasVideoMarketing`, wired correctly |

**Who gets access now (correctly):**
- **Pro** → always has access (plan check + auto-set on purchase)
- **Elite** → always has access (plan check + auto-set on purchase)
- **Growth + Video Marketing bundle** → `hasVideoMarketing: true` is now saved on checkout return, so they get through
- **Free / Starter / Growth without video** → see the full landing page you asked for

content.thinking:
Found a subtle issue! The `/api/auth/me` endpoint does:
```javascript
const { password, ...safeUser } = req.user as any;
```

It uses `req.user` which is the **session user** (passport's user object, deserialized from the session). This is NOT a fresh DB read. The session user might not have the latest `hasVideoMarketing` value if it was updated after the session was created.

The `confirm-plan` route DOES return the fresh user from DB:


```javascript
const updated = await storage.getUser(userId);
const { password: _, ...safe } = updated!;
res.json(safe);
```

And `SelectPlan.tsx` does:
```javascript
queryClient.setQueryData(["/api/auth/me"], updated);
```

So after plan confirmation, the React Query cache is updated with the fresh user data. So in the same session, right after plan confirmation, `hasVideoMarketing` will be in the cache correctly.

But in a NEW session (user logs out and logs back in), `/api/auth/me` uses `req.user` from the passport session deserialization. If passport doesn't refresh the user object when deserializing the session, the stale `hasVideoMarketing` value will persist until the session is refreshed or the user logs out and back in again.

Let me trace through how passport handles deserialization to see if it's pulling fresh data from the database or just using what's already in the session store.

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user (admin/owner of Oravini platform) wanted to:
   - Copy the OraviniLanding page style/structure and apply it to the Video Marketing section so that when users click on "Video Marketing" they see a full, professional landing page

   - Fix "Get Started" CTAs that were looping logged-in users back to the main landing page instead of taking them into the platform
   - Make the non-access landing page (TierGate) look exactly like OraviniLanding — particle canvas, "Welcome to ORAVINI VIDEO MARKETING", all sections, features, how it works, pricing

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - React/TypeScript SPA with Wouter routing
   - Express.js backend with Passport.js session auth
   - Drizzle ORM with PostgreSQL
   - Access control based on `user.plan` ("free", "starter", "growth", "pro", "elite") and `user.hasVideoMarketing` boolean
   - Particle canvas animation (gold particles, mouse interaction)
   - IntersectionObserver for scroll animations and counter animations
   - TiltCard 3D perspective hover effect
   - React Query cache management for user state (`queryClient.setQueryData`)
   - Passport `deserializeUser` does fresh DB read on every request
   - Whop checkout integration for plan purchases
   - Guard HOC pattern for protected routes in App.tsx

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`client/src/components/video-marketing/TierGate.tsx`** (complete rewrite twice)
     - First version: basic Oravini-style page with particle canvas, 6 feature cards, pricing
     - Final version: full copy of OraviniLanding structure — particle canvas hero with "Welcome to / ORAVINI / VIDEO MARKETING / Powered by Brandverse", useScrollAnim, Counter, TiltCard, Anim wrapper, Navbar with Features/Pricing/Dashboard/Upgrade buttons, 3-pillar platform section, stats strip, 12-feature grid, 4-step how-it-works, outcomes with animated bars, 3-tier pricing (Growth+addon → /video-marketing-addon, Pro → /select-plan, Elite → /select-plan), footer
     - Key export: `hasVideoMarketingAccess(plan, hasVideoMarketing)` — returns true for "pro", "elite", or "growth" + hasVideoMarketing=true
     ```typescript
     export function hasVideoMarketingAccess(plan: string | undefined | null, hasVideoMarketing: boolean = false): boolean {
         if (!plan) return false;
         if (plan === "pro" || plan === "elite") return true;
         if (plan === "growth" && hasVideoMarketing) return true;
         return false;
     }
     interface TierGateProps {
         currentPlan?: string;
         userName?: string;
         hasVideoMarketing?: boolean;
     }
     ```

   - **`client/src/pages/client/VideoMarketing.tsx`** (modified)
     - Added admin bypass so admins always reach PlatformView
     - Fixed field: `user.hasVideoMarketingAddon` (never set) → `(user as any).hasVideoMarketing`
     ```typescript
     const isAdmin = (user as any).role === "admin";
     if (!isAdmin && !hasVideoMarketingAccess(user.plan, (user as any).hasVideoMarketing)) {
         return <TierGate currentPlan={user.plan} userName={user.name} hasVideoMarketing={(user as any).hasVideoMarketing} />;
     }
     return <PlatformView />;
     ```

   - **`server/routes.ts`** — `confirm-plan` route (modified)
     - Was only saving `plan`, dropping `hasVideoMarketing` from request body
     - Fixed to also save `hasVideoMarketing`, and auto-set to `true` for Pro/Elite
     ```typescript
     const { plan, hasVideoMarketing } = req.body;
     const update: any = { planConfirmed: true };
     if (plan && ["free", "starter", "growth", "pro", "elite"].includes(plan)) update.plan = plan;
     if (typeof hasVideoMarketing === "boolean") update.hasVideoMarketing = hasVideoMarketing;
     if (update.plan === "pro" || update.plan === "elite") update.hasVideoMarketing = true;
     ```

   - **`client/src/components/video-marketing/PublicNav.tsx`** (rewritten)
     - Logo: `/` → `/video-marketing`
     - "Get Started" → `/login` changed to "Access Platform" → `/video-marketing`

   - **`client/src/components/video-marketing/Hero.tsx`** (modified)
     - "Start Free — Login to Access" → `/login` changed to "Access the Platform" → `/video-marketing`

   - **`client/src/components/video-marketing/FinalCTA.tsx`** (rewritten)
     - Both CTAs: `/login` → `/video-marketing`
     - Primary button: "Access the Platform", secondary: "Get Started Free"

   - **`client/src/components/video-marketing/PricingCTA.tsx`** (modified)
     - All plan CTAs: `<Link href="/login">` → `<Link href="/video-marketing">`

   - **`client/src/components/video-marketing/PlatformView.tsx`** (read-only, verified)
     - Fully built with 14 tabs across two sections
     - WEBINAR_NAV: Live Webinars, Series, Landing Pages, CRM, Recordings, Email Sequences, Analytics, API & Settings
     - HOSTING_NAV: Library, VSL Studio, Collections, VSL Library, Analytics, Viewer CRM, Player Settings
     - Section switcher: "webinars" | "video-hosting"

   - **`server/index.ts`** (verified)
     - `deserializeUser` does fresh `storage.getUser(id)` DB read on every request — ensures `hasVideoMarketing` is always current

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **`hasVideoMarketingAddon` vs `hasVideoMarketing` mismatch**: `VideoMarketing.tsx` was checking `user.hasVideoMarketingAddon` (a field nothing in the codebase ever sets) instead of `user.hasVideoMarketing`. Fixed by switching to `(user as any).hasVideoMarketing`.
